subject: Re: TeamTomo and latest Warp/M versions?
body:
Hi Alister,

 Thanks for the response.  With our installation of Warp 1.09 we used the
"Browse Public Repository" to pull down some pretrained models (
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=135) and placed them in the C:\Program
Files\Warp\boxnet2models as separate folders with the models. Other models
pulled from the "Browse Public Repository" ran without issue here. Example:
BoxNet2Mask_2018_0531 could run without issue here.

 We will plan to keep progressing forward as this may not be a critical
step for all parts of the tutorial.

 I was asking with the GoldNet model in case we were being hit with a bug
that is fixed in a newer build of Warp/M such as a newer Tensorflow
distribution, etc. We've had some other issues with stability and crashing,
including some harder crashes (blue screens) from processing that we are
trying to work through.

 -Matt

On Tue, Jan 4, 2022 at 1:14 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Matt,
>
> I don’t think there is a newer build available - sorry about the problems
> with the GoldNet. Do you get the same errors with the pretrained model
> included in your installation?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 4 Jan 2022, at 19:06, Matt Larson <larsonmattr@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>
> We are trying to complete the https://teamtomo.org tutorial for
> EMPIAR-10164 (HIV VLPs) which combines Warp-RELION-M and Dynamo.
>
>  Our environment is a Windows 10 PC with a single NVIDIA 1080 GPU. We are
> running the Warp 1.0.9 version, with the 'copy and replace' of files using
> the Warp nightly build 2020-11-04.  I believe this is the latest available
> release on the website of http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=65
>
>  Prior to using the 2020-11-04 nightly build, we had crashes with the GUI
> during initial preprocessing. The use of the nightly build code appears to
> have resolved these.
>
>  Now we are hitting an error when using the TeamTomo GoldNet model, where
> a TensorFlow.TFException: Attempting to use uninitialized
> value.conv2d_25/kernel, causes the step (
> https://teamtomo.org/walkthroughs/EMPIAR-10164/2d-preprocessing.html) to
> fail when using Pick Particles with the GoldNet model.  This step is to
> mask out beads prior to tomographic reconstruction.
>
>  Before we go further to diagnose and troubleshoot Warp/M, is there a
> newer total build beyond the 2020-11-04?  We would like to work with the
> latest stable version of Warp and ideally the same version as TeamTomo has
> used to test the workflows.
>
>  If anyone can point out where we can get the latest build for Warp with
> or without an installer, please let me know!
>
>  Thanks,
>   Matt
>
>  ------------------------
>
> *Matt Larson*
>
> Associate Scientist
>
> Computer Scientist/System Administrator
>
> Cryo-EM Research Center
>
> University of Wisconsin Madison
>
> 440 Henry Mall. Room 1220
>
> Madison, WI 53706
